block_6_diagnostic.favorable_characteristics: n_features_vs_ablation_delta: rho=0.5167; is_classification_vs_SC-OTS_v1_delta: rho=0.8528; is_classification_vs_SC-OTS_v2_delta: rho=0.8528; interaction_order_vs_SC-OTS_v1_delta: rho=-0.802; interaction_order_vs_SC-OTS_v2_delta: rho=-0.6786

per_subclaim.conclusion: SC-OTS v1 has avg rank 8.0 vs RO-FIGS 8.2. SC-OTS ranks better across 10 datasets.

per_subclaim.claim: SC-OTS uses fewer splits than RO-FIGS
per_subclaim.key_statistic: SC-OTS=20 splits, RO-FIGS=20 splits, Pareto count=0
per_subclaim.conclusion: SC-OTS v1 avg 20 splits vs RO-FIGS 20. SC-OTS on Pareto frontier for 0/10 datasets.

per_subclaim.key_statistic: Mean enhanced dCor F1 = 0.748 across 4 synthetic datasets
per_subclaim.conclusion: Enhanced interaction dCor achieves mean F1=0.748. Strong on friedman1/3, weaker on synth_3way/4way with higher-order interactions.
Faithfulness precision averages 100.0% across synthetic datasets with SHAP ground truth. Exceeds the 70% target.
Bootstrap CI for SIMPLICIAL-UNCONSTRAINED spans [-0.0787, 0.0618]. Straddles zero — no reliable advantage. P(simplicial > unconstrained) = 41.4%.
XGBoost+SC vs XGBoost: pooled Cohen's d=-0.681, p=0.0019. Effect is large.
SC-OTS v1 rank 8.0 vs best FIGS rank 5.9. CD=4.28.
SC-OTS rank gap to XGBoost/EBM is 6.0 (CD=4.28). Beyond critical difference.

per_subclaim.key_statistic: 10/10 datasets with Betti analysis, 0 with Betti-1 > 0
per_subclaim.conclusion: Betti numbers computed for 10/10 datasets. Betti-0 differentiates feature clusters; Betti-1 mostly 0 (no circular dependencies). Topological summary provides compact interpretable representation.

block_7_verdict.synthesis: The SC-OTS hypothesis is partially confirmed. 5/9 sub-claims confirmed, 2 against, 2 inconclusive. SC-OTS achieves competitive accuracy with RO-FIGS and produces interpretable topological structures, but the ablation study shows that simplicial constraints do not reliably improve over unconstrained splits (CI straddles zero), undermining the core mechanistic claim. The enhanced interaction dCor method shows strong recovery on simple synthetic datasets, but degrades on higher-order interactions.
